source.title: Anthropic Education Report: The AI Fluency Index

viewerQuestion: Why does a polished first AI output need more active review, not less?
centralThesis: Polish is not proof. Treat the first output as the start of a collaboration loop, then verify goal fit, missing context, evidence, real-world performance, and final human ownership before use.
reusableArtifact: High-Quality Collaboration Turn Checklist: Goal, Gaps, Grounds, Test, Owner

P0: A finished-looking output can suppress critical evaluation
A high-quality AI turn ends with an inspected, tested, owned result rather than an accepted first draft
The five-pass checklist is the reusable method
P1: Use iteration as a deliberate review method
Set collaboration terms before the answer arrives
Check goal fit and missing context
Verify grounds and test the artifact
Keep a named human owner for consequences and release
P2: Anthropic sample size and observable-behavior methodology
85.7 percent iteration prevalence and the 2.67 versus 1.33 behavior comparison
5.6 times reasoning-question and 4 times missing-context associations
P3Removed: Institutional history unrelated to the viewer's review problem
Repeated descriptions of the taxonomy that add no review action
Speculative causal claims not supported by the study

chapters.label: Introduction
chapters.promise: See why visual polish can hide an unfinished reasoning process

chapters.label: The Polish Trap
chapters.promise: Recognize when a finished-looking artifact suppresses evaluation

chapters.label: Iteration
chapters.promise: Use follow-up turns to expose assumptions and improve the work

chapters.label: Set the Terms
chapters.promise: Define how the AI should challenge, explain, and signal uncertainty

chapters.label: Inspect the Work
chapters.promise: Check goal fit, context, grounds, and real-world performance

chapters.label: Review Loop
chapters.promise: Run one reusable five-pass checklist before release

chapters.label: Summary
chapters.promise: Turn polished drafts into inspected and owned results

visualSemantics.humanRole: defines acceptance, challenges the draft, tests the result, and owns release
visualSemantics.agentRole: produces a draft, exposes assumptions, gathers evidence, revises, and reports uncertainty
visualSemantics.artifact: one polished result card moving through a five-pass review loop

factBoundaries: The findings are correlational, not causal
The sample is one week of substantive Claude.ai multi-turn conversations
Only 11 of 24 framework behaviors were observable inside chat
Testing and judgment may occur outside the conversation
Polish is a review trigger, not evidence that the output is wrong

contentEdit.keptEvidence: All P0 and P1 material plus every P2 item that establishes credibility, mechanism, transfer, or a boundary
contentEdit.removedOnly: Repeated taxonomy description, institutional background, and speculative or generic language with no additional fact, boundary, step, or understanding value
